text.content: "The Minis are collecting items. Help count what they found."

text.content: 2.1  Scale Comparison 1 5 Vs  [s2_1_scale_comparison_1_5_vs]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Scale Comparison 1 5 Vs  [s2_1_scale_comparison_1_5_vs]

text.content: Left vertical picture graph appears. Student's data shown with scale of 5. Key: each symbol = 5.

rich_text.plain_text: Left vertical picture graph appears. Student's data shown with scale of 5. Key: each symbol = 5.

text.content: Right vertical picture graph appears. Same data shown with scale of 10. Key: each symbol = 10.

rich_text.plain_text: Right vertical picture graph appears. Same data shown with scale of 10. Key: each symbol = 10.

text.content: "Here's your data two ways. Look at both graphs and notice the scale of each. Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's your data two ways. Look at both graphs and notice the scale of each. Which graph uses fewer symbols to show the same data?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Left graph (scale of 5)", "Right graph (scale of 10)"]
"Which graph uses FEWER symbols to show the same data?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: ["Left graph (scale of 5)", "Right graph (scale of 10)"]
"Which graph uses FEWER symbols to show the same data?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected the 1:10 graph, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected the 1:10 graph, correct

text.content: Right graph highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. Scale of 10 uses fewer symbols. Each symbol shows more, so you need fewer of them."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. Scale of 10 uses fewer symbols. Each symbol shows more, so you need fewer of them."

text.content: ❌ Student selected the 1:5 graph, which uses more symbols

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected the 1:5 graph, which uses more symbols

text.content: Left graph highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Left graph highlights.

text.content: Right graph highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights.

text.content: "Look at the keys. When each symbol stands for more, you need fewer symbols for the same number counted. Which key shows a bigger number?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Look at the keys. When each symbol stands for more, you need fewer symbols for the same number counted. Which key shows a bigger number?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Right graph highlights to show the answer.

rich_text.plain_text: Right graph highlights to show the answer.

text.content: "Let me explain this comparison. The right graph has scale of 10. Each symbol stands for 10. The left graph has scale of 5. Each symbol stands for 5. When each symbol shows more, you need fewer symbols to show the same amount. The right graph uses fewer symbols because its scale is bigger."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me explain this comparison. The right graph has scale of 10. Each symbol stands for 10. The left graph has scale of 5. Each symbol stands for 5. When each symbol shows more, you need fewer symbols to show the same amount. The right graph uses fewer symbols because its scale is bigger."

text.content: "With scale of 10, each symbol represents more than scale of 5. That's why you need fewer symbols for the same data."

rich_text.plain_text: "With scale of 10, each symbol represents more than scale of 5. That's why you need fewer symbols for the same data."

text.content: "Let me explain. Yellow on the picture graph has 4 symbols. The key shows each symbol equals 10, so that is 40 total: 10, 20, 30, 40. Both show the same data."

text.content: "Bar graphs are really useful with scale of 10. You can read the bar HEIGHT on a vertical graph instead of counting symbols. You can read exact amounts from the numbers on the side. Today you will even figure out what the bar means when it stops between numbers."
